ead.archdesc:
======= RMM02548Gustav Jaeger Requardt papers2548Compiled by D. TralkaDivision of Rare and Manuscript Collections
May 20042B Carl A. Kroch LibraryCornell UniversityIthaca, NY 14853rareref@cornell.eduURL:
physc euro phtclThis finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2022-09-19 18:29:28 UTC.12/07/2018
This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)2007-05-29
converted from EAD 1.0 to EAD 2002 >>>>>>> 2e3ec5c57314077b8435567632a49d313357daab
English
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections
Gustav Jaeger Requardt papers
Requardt, Gustav J. (Gustav Jaeger), 1886-1978.
2548
1.8 cubic feet. 1.8 cubic feet.
1905-1966. Correspondence, diary, reports, certificates, clippings, photographs, press releases. Printed and photocopied items, mainly concerning Requardt's student years at Cornell University, his participation in alumni activities, his career in sanitary engineering, and his interest in national politics and public affairs. Collection material in English 2080086
General
Contact Information:
Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu
Compiled by:
D. Tralka
Date completed:
1983
EAD encoding:
Ann Hubert, May 2004
BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
Engineer.
Cornell University Class of 1909.
COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
Correspondence; diary (1962) of Requardt's tour of Scandinavia, Holland, and West Germeny; reports; certificates; clippings; photographs; press releases; biographical sketches on the Requardt family; and printed and photocopied items, mainly concerning Requardt's student years at Cornell University, his participation in alumni activities, his career in sanitary engineering, and his interest in national politics and public affairs. Includes material relating to his partnership in Whitman, Requardt, and Associates (Baltimore, Maryland). Chief correspondents include John Marshall Butler, George H. Fallon, and Herbert R. O'Conor.
Cite As:
Gustav Jaeger Requardt papers, #2548. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.
Photographs. Diaries. West Germany -- Description and travel. Scandinavia -- Description and travel. Netherlands -- Description and travel. Civil engineers. Voyages and travels. Sanitary engineering. Civil engineering. Politics, Practical. Butler, John Marshall. Fallon, George H. O'Conor, Herbert R. Requardt family. Cornell University -- : Alumni and alumnae. Cornell University -- : Students. Whitman, Requardt, and Associates
